titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/212

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=480 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-12 13:45:36.068657+00 | translation moa=479 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-12 13:45:36.086754+00

div.p:
Omnibus Has Litteras usiuris uel Audituris Tam presentibus quam futuris . Patricius Comes de Dunbar Salutem . Noueritis
nos Concessisse et Hac presenti Carta nostra Confirmasse Deo et Ecclesie Sancte Marie De Melros et Monachis
ibidem Deo Seriuentibus omnes Donationes et Conuentiones et Concessiones quas Iohannes De Methkil ipsis fecit
De terra De Pannesceles cum Omnibus pertinentiis suis et Libertatibus et Aesimentis Eidem terre De Pannesceles
interius et exterius iuste pertinentibus Tenendas et Habendas ipsis in perpetuum . de Dicto Iohanne De Methkil et Heredibus
suis secundum Tenorem Cartarum quas ipsi Monachi Habent de Dicto Iohanne super dicta terra saluo seruicio nostro
de Iohanne filio Walleui . Hiis Testibus . Patricio filio nostro . Willelmo filio nostro . Walter . Olifard . Iusticiario Laodonie . Bernardo
Fraser . Adam de Paulewurth . Rogero de Merlay . Willelmo de Bolteby Alano filio Alani filio Edgari et Patricio
fratre eius Tunc temporis clerico Comitis.

p.cl: To all who shall see or hear these letters, as well present as to come, Patrick Earl of Dunbar sends greeting.

Know us to have granted and by this our present charter to have made firm to God and the church of St Mary of Melrose and the monks in that place serving God, all gifts, agreements, and grants, which John of Methil made to them respecting the land of Penshiel, with all its pertinents, liberties, and easements justly pertaining to the said land of Penshiel, within and without. For them to be held and possessed for ever of the said John of Methil and his heirs according to the tenor of the charters which the monks have from the said John over the said land, saving our service from John son of Waldef.

With these witnesses: Patrick our son; William our son; Walter Oliphant, justiciar of Lothian; Bernard Fraser; Adam of Polwarth; Roger of Merlay; William of Boltby; Alan, son of Alan son of Edgar, and Patrick his brother, at that time the earl's clerk.
